item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is any of this cute??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so messed up. Please put that thing out of its misery. This is the ultimate stalking victim's nightmare. That poor girl…

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Agreed with this. Comes off incredibly jarring.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Obviously I'm a little late on this one, but it does it really make sense to implied a shared origin there when the origin of 427 is both known and extremely difficult to replicate since both creation components are the Foundation's? Noting structural similarities between the produced entities would be enough of a tie without going into somewhat immersion breaking territory imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus this was excellent, one of the closest to life depictions of interfamily horror on the site. Reminded me of all sorts of irl murder and kidnapping stories. Poor Monica…

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

In reading this article, my mind was brought to SCP-427 and the "flesh beasts" it creates. Since the Foundation created 427, its likely the rings from 2151 are not some random accident as well. Additionally, the mention of Site-81 in the containment procedures could be linked to the Site-81 Dossier to provide the most bang for one's wikiwalk buck. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-2151-A and SCP-2151-B are to be kept in separate high security containment lockers at Site-81. Testing of SCP-2151-A and -B is currently suspended.

To this:

SCP-2151-A and SCP-2151-B are to be kept in separate high security containment lockers at Site-81. Testing of SCP-2151-A and -B is currently suspended.

Changing this:

The point of contact between the two subjects will also increase in temperature, often resulting in severe burning in the area, and the skin there will fuse. As time passes, more and more of the skin connecting the two individuals will become fused, eventually consuming the entire bodies of both subjects. Over time, the distinguishing characteristics of the individual subjects will become distorted and unrecognizable from their original state, and many of the biological functions of the individuals will shut down entirely. The resulting entity is classified as SCP-2151-1(X). It is currently uncertain whether any higher mental functions are maintained throughout this process.

To this:

The point of contact between the two subjects will also increase in temperature, often resulting in severe burning in the area, and the skin there will fuse. As time passes, more and more of the skin connecting the two individuals will become fused, eventually consuming the entire bodies of both subjects. Over time, the distinguishing characteristics of the individual subjects will become distorted and unrecognizable from their original state, and many of the biological functions of the individuals will shut down entirely1. The resulting entity is classified as SCP-2151-1(X). It is currently uncertain whether any higher mental functions are maintained throughout this process.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarity in this process to the creation of instances of SCP-427-1 has been noted. The potential of a similar origin for SCP-2151-A and -B,and SCP-427 is currently under investigation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just gross and disgusting and unsettling

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really good job. Don't know why, but I like it. But can you please tell me what scp-2151-1A looks like? +1 by the way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHH take your fucking upvote. Ew ew ew. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the new picture for this one. Gives off a more interesting mood.

item.title: Photo credits
item.content:encoded:
the photo of the girl is:

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Los_Angeles_girl_1973.jpg

and is public domain, if the uploader got the details right which is not guaranteed.

the ring is credited in the first post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kaiser Crab: for me it's not a hole, it's what makes the skip so good.
Easy to go from 'we won't tell you' to 'You don't want to know'. And it makes everything much worse.
Upvoted, of course!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WTF upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how much this accomplishes in such a short article, but there's a BIG hole here that's preventing me from upvoting…

There's almost NO info about SCP-2151-1A beyond the fact that it's still alive. Is it sentient? If so, what is its behavior? Can it talk? If so, why are there no interviews?

I was unclear if the skip says the resultant -1(X) instances are all brain dead or if that refers to the wearers as the effect advances, or is only referring to unsuccessful -1(X)s… It could use some clarification.

No vote for now, but it's close to an upvote, regarding the above issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This does have a host of faults, mentioned above and others (what about forcible separation? Tearing skin isn't hard and I imagine my reaction to someone trying to merge like this with me would be to try and get it the fuck off…. but then, he might have subdued her and… urgh so it gets a pass) but the result works so … well that I'm forced to upvote anyhow.
Because urgh, this is how you do creepy and unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, now this was really disgusting.

I somehow am saying that in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty creepy article, although I feel the final note could be a little more subtle. Upvoted nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just had a thought while rereading this one, that actually manages to up the fridge horror even more:

What if this isn't what the devices were meant to do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this suggestion is horrible, but consider removing the picture of Monica so we don't have a lower bound for her age.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I stated earlier, this is unsettling in an extremely good way, but I have one issue: In the statement about how much genetic material Monica and the unnamed guy share, the redacted percentage has two digits. According to wikipedia, humans are on average 99.9% similar to each other genetically. You might want to change that to something like "and shared at least 99.██% of their genetic material with Monica ████████."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, that's not completely fucking creepy and also slightly terrifying at all! Why do you ask?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The basic concept is certainly inspired in old lore, but you took it even further, make it even more gutwrenching as time went.

Good job, evokes you know who.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aye, good call with that. I can make those changes.

Edit: Made a couple of edits based on feedback from Michael Atreus and Armstrong. Think it reads a bit better now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an extremely good concept. The idea of completely merging two different organic beings into a functioning entity (even though extremely short-lived) is really something else. However, no matter how creative it is, the way it is written is bothering me enough for me not to vote on this just yet.

While there are no huge grammar errors, sentences like ("These objects have not been crafted using anomalous materials.") and ("No other anomalous activity occurs past this point.") are not necessary at all. If the material is anomalous, it would be said if it was known anyway. The same goes for the ceasing of anomalous activity.

The addendum is really creepy, though. Congratulations on the execution, though you might want to put the text in a code block with the image block code over it so it's better formatted. This is done on SCP-2172 and i think you could copy the source code with no major problems.

Edit: Due to the recent edits, i have changed my vote accordingly. It is now an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably what they mean by this is that the objects are made of things like steel and copper rather than modified telekill alloy or an advanced metamaterial. Any properties they have either come from some bizarre and extremely advanced application of standard physics and biology (the anomaly being that the device was created in some guy's garage), or from some as yet undetermined source (aka "not saying the 'magic' word, but…")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of another draft I saw on chat. But anyways, the end note tied the creep factor off. I feel bad for Monica…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Terrific concept. The addendum was perfectly crafted. Shudder. However, certain elements of the description were ambiguous in a way that I found confusing.

These objects have not been crafted using anomalous materials.

This is an unusual sentence to find in a Description. The rings themselves are clearly anomalous. Presumably, no one from the Foundation witnessed their creation. You've already said they are stainless steel: but not magic stainless steel? How does one determine the difference?

The temperature/burning/fusing thing - I think the Foundation would be a little more specific. Are we talking third-degree cauterization fusing, or more like a first degree burn with a biological intergrowth? Both sound pretty awful to me, only my brain doesn't know which image to run with.

I think maybe Monica should be capitalized in the transliteration of the note (or else 'my' uncapitalized').

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept itself was interesting enough to keep me reading, but what got me was the ending note. Good pictures, and I definitely didn't expect the creepiness.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how the twist isn't completely shoved into our faces, except maybe for the letter, which I feel isn't really needed that much. Aside from that, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
genetic material collected from SCP-2151-1A has shown that the subject was likely approximately 50 years of age, and shared at least ██% of their genetic material with Monica ████████

…oh fucking god.

As much as I hate to say it, +1. Jesus H. Fuckstick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so cute! Take an upvote and a :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This brought back some terrible, terrible memories. Take my upvote and leave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: creepy and disgusting, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that was chilling. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that 2151-1A isn't described, gives a nice gap to put horrible imagination into. But I wonder if perhaps more detail about how it's to be maintained might kick up the "ohgahdwhatisit" factor a bit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a viciously unpleasant little SCP there. It's well written, and has that right amount of gore and squick without going too overboard.

+1, and, blech

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's hesitation in me upvoting due to the nature of the twist you bring in the end and how much squick it produces, but considering the angle you're going for and how it's played against the already unsettling effects of the SCP, it deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Slightly creepy and disturbing. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something short that I had stuck in my sandbox for a while and finally decided to finish up. My thanks to Decibelle for checking it out briefly in chat.

Hope you guys enjoy it!

Edit: Image source is from Wikimedia Commons, licensed under CC.
